title: What's new with data agents
summary: Google Cloud presented new tools for building data analytics agents, including the Agent Development Kit (ADK) and Model Context Protocol (MCP) integrations. These tools are accessible via Gemini CLI, Claude Code, Codex, and IDEs like VSCode to streamline workflows in data engineering, data science, and conversational analytics.
writeup: Google Cloud Tech presents new tooling for building data analytics agents, centered on the Agent Development Kit (ADK) and Model Context Protocol (MCP) integrations. The integrations are reachable through Gemini CLI, Claude Code, Codex, and IDEs such as VSCode, extending agentic workflows into existing development environments. The stated aim is to streamline work across data engineering, data science, and conversational analytics. The takeaway: ADK and MCP serve as the building blocks for these data agents, and the supported clients determine where they can be used.
